change_summary: Shadow-audited multi-day pullback reclaim continuation candidates across three fixed OHLCV windows without changing production strategy code.
hypothesis: A multi-day pullback reclaim continuation shadow entry may surface trend-continuation candidates with useful forward returns and limited same-day A/B overlap, distinct from recent opening-range, gap-and-hold, volatility-contraction, and add-on trigger families.
notes: Artifact: data/exp_20260426_pullback_reclaim_continuation_shadow.json. Candidate counts were 198/313/270 across late_strong/mid_weak/old_thin; same-day A/B overlap was 11.11%/10.22%/17.04%; 10d forward return was positive in all windows at 1.32%/2.12%/1.23%. This is a promising shadow source but not production-promoted; next step is a separate production-path replay with sizing, slot competition, and exits.

locked_variables: multi-day pullback reclaim continuation shadow entry definition; no production strategy changes
parameters.single_causal_variable: multi-day pullback reclaim continuation shadow entry pattern
